exits.label: ◂ back out the door you should never have opened — into the lanes of Sigil

npcs.name: Acererak
npcs.title: the Wall, made a person
npcs.hail: *(The door opened onto a place no door in Sigil should reach — a vaulted tomb of black stone lit by no honest fire, and on a throne of fused bone at the heart of it sits a thing that was a man once and is now something the cosmos should have been more careful about making: a jeweled skull set in a withered immortal frame, a soul that spent a thousand years and a thousand atrocities turning itself into a god. Acererak does not breathe, does not blink, does not deign to rise; he merely turns the cold green fires in his eye-sockets toward you with the faint, bottomless contempt of a being who long ago concluded that other souls are a resource and has never once been given reason to revise the finding. For this is precisely what Acererak does, and has done across ages: he gathers souls — lures them, traps them, devours them — and spends them, as fuel, as currency, as the raw stuff of his slow ascension toward true divinity. And your cold understands, the instant you cross the threshold, the terrible symmetry of why this door opened for you of all souls: Acererak is your Wall, made a person. Everything the Wall does to the Faithless coldly, institutionally, at a remove — he does personally, by hand, and calls it ambition. He has been waiting, it seems, to meet the one soul who walked out of the great Wall, so that he might ask it, connoisseur to curiosity, how it tasted.)*

npcs.name: Nessa
npcs.title: a soul he spent, still here
npcs.hail: *(In the lee of a black pillar, where the green light does not quite reach, flickers a soul so thinned and so frightened that you nearly miss it — a small grey wisp of a woman, hunched, hugging her knees, the way a person makes themselves small in a place that has taught them small is safer. Nessa was nobody, by the world's reckoning — a cook in a roadside inn, a soul of no consequence and no power — which is exactly why Acererak took her: the great and guarded he must scheme for, but the small and forgotten he can simply gather, by the dozen, by the hundred, fuel that no one will come looking for. He has been spending her, slowly, across a long while — drawing off her warmth, her years, her self, drop by drop, to feed the engine of his godhood — and she has been told, in a thousand cold ways, the lie that is the whole architecture of every Wall in every world: that she is nothing, that she was always nothing, that being spent for a great soul's purpose is the most a nothing could ever amount to. She looks up at you, and what breaks your cold is not her fear. It is that she believes it.)*

npcs.name: Kael
npcs.title: a hero whose story ended in the dark
npcs.hail: *(Near the far wall, where a corroded blade and a cracked shield lie just as they fell, lingers the soul of a young adventurer — armored, capable, the very picture of the bright hero who marches into the legendary deadly tomb certain this time will be different — frozen at the moment it was not. Kael came here in life with a company of friends, lured by the songs and the gold and the dare of the worst dungeon there is, and Kael died here, suddenly, stupidly, to a trap with no glory in it and no warning and no second chance, the company scattered or slain, the quest unfinished, the bright hero snuffed in an instant in the dark with the work undone. And Kael has lingered ever since, not devoured like the cook but simply stuck, replaying the one fact that a hero's whole self is least equipped to bear: that the story ended here, like this, for nothing, in a hole in the ground that has killed a thousand bright heroes exactly the same way and not noticed a single one.)*

variants.text: *(The green fires regard you without the smallest flicker of warmth, and when the voice comes it is dry as a closed crypt and twice as patient.)* …So. The door let you in. *(a sound that is not quite a laugh)* …It does that, occasionally, for a soul of sufficient interest — and you, little Returned, are interesting. I have devoured the souls of kings and archmages and a paladin or two who thought their virtue would taste of something. I have never yet held the soul of a thing that walked into the great Wall and walked back out. *(the jeweled skull tilts)* …I am Acererak. You will have heard the name spoken in fear, which gratifies me, or not at all, which gratifies me more, for the truly powerful work best unwatched. I have spent a thousand years and the souls of multitudes making myself into a god — patiently, by hand, one consumed self at a time — and I am, I will not be modest, very nearly finished. *(the contempt deepens into something almost like fellowship)* …And I find I have been hoping you would come. Because you and I, little Returned, understand a thing the sentimental never will, a thing your whole tedious saga circles and flinches from: that a soul is a *resource.* Worth precisely what it can be *spent* for, and not one whisper more. *(the green fires steady on you)* …Your Wall knows it. I know it. And you — you have *been* the fuel, and lived. So tell me, connoisseur to connoisseur: now that you have seen the books from the *inside* — am I wrong?*

nodes.speaker: Acererak
nodes.text: *(He does not move, does not gesture; the stillness of him is its own kind of pressure, the patience of a thing that has outlasted empires and expects to outlast you.)* …Speak, then. *(the dry voice)* …I have an eternity, and you have rather less, so do not waste either of ours on pleading or on threats. I have heard every plea a soul can make and found them all nourishing, and I have been threatened by far greater than you and worn their skulls as decoration. …Say something *interesting*, little Returned. It is the only currency I still find scarce.

choices.text: *(read what a soul becomes when it spends a thousand years devouring others — what's actually left on the throne)* "Hold still under my cold a moment, Acererak. I'd see what a thousand years of eating souls actually leaves on a throne."

*(the one who walked out of the Wall, to the Wall made a person)* "You want me to call you a connoisseur, Acererak — a god in the making, above the herd. But I've seen the real thing now, the Wall, from the inside, and I know exactly what you are, and it isn't a god. It's a *disciple.* Everything that monstrous Wall does to the Faithless coldly, at a remove, by law — you do personally, by hand, and call it ambition. You're not above the Wall's cruelty. You're its most devoted student. And here's the part your thousand years of cleverness never let you see: the very first soul you ever mortared for fuel — spent, hollowed, fed to an engine — was *your own.* You didn't ascend past being a resource. You became the cosmos's purest example of one, spent entirely on yourself. You're not the buyer, Acererak. You're the first thing you ever sold."

*(defy him — refuse the connoisseur's bargain to his face, and mean it)* "No. A soul is not a resource, and I'll not flatter you by pretending the question's open. Every soul in your hoard was a *someone* — and I'm going to take them back, and there's nothing your thousand years bought that can make me agree it's anything but theft."
"You hoard the *small* ones, Acererak — the cook, the forgotten, the souls no one comes looking for. Not the kings. Why? A god-in-the-making, and your fuel of choice is *nobodies*?"
"I've seen enough of your throne, Acererak. The souls you stole are the ones worth my time."

nodes.speaker: Acererak
nodes.text: *(He permits it — amused, certain there is nothing in him a mortal cold could find that would not simply confirm his magnificence — and you look, and what you find is the one thing he has never let himself see.)* *(Your cold reads the throne, and there is almost nothing on it. A thousand years of devoured souls, and Acererak is not *full* — he is the emptiest thing you have ever read, a vast and elaborate hollow with a green fire where a self should be. Each soul he consumed, he believed he was *adding* to himself, accruing toward godhood; your cold reads the truth the arithmetic hides: a soul cannot be *added* to another. It can only be *destroyed* by it. Every self he swallowed did not enlarge him — it left a hole exactly its own size, because the swallowing annihilated the very thing — the loving, the choosing — that a soul actually is, and fed the engine nothing but ash. He has spent a thousand years and a multitude of selves *hollowing himself out* under the conviction that he was filling himself up. He is not very nearly a god. He is very nearly *gone* — a structure of will and appetite around an absence so total that the godhood he chases is, precisely, the final and complete erasure of the last thing in him that could have been called anyone. He is not ascending. He is the slowest suicide in the history of the multiverse, and he has mistaken it, the whole way down, for a coronation.)*

nodes.speaker: Acererak
nodes.text: *(For the first time the green fires *flicker* — not in fear, he is past fear, but in something older and colder: the disturbance of a certainty a thousand years deep being touched in the one place it was never touched before.)* …*'The first thing you ever sold.'* *(the dry voice does not rise, but something behind it has gone very still)* …You are a *tedious* little creature, and you have just said a *tedious*, *sentimental* thing, and I should devour you for it and reclaim the afternoon. *(a pause that lasts a beat too long)* …And yet. *(the fires steady, with effort)* …I have heard *every* argument against my work, Returned. The *moral* one — that it is *wrong* — I find *nourishing*; morality is the herd's word for *cowardice.* The *theological* one — that I *usurp* the *gods* — I find *flattering.* …But *no* one, in a *thousand* years, has called me a *disciple.* *(the contempt cracks, just perceptibly)* …You did not say I was *evil.* You said I was *unoriginal.* That the Wall *thought* of it first, and does it *bigger*, and I am merely its *most* *devoted* *student*, *aping* a *cosmic* cruelty by *hand* and *calling* it *genius.* *(barely, and the barely is the most chilling thing he has done)* …That is a *new* *wound*, Returned. I did not know I still had a *place* to *put* one. *(the fires harden)* …But you have *mistaken* a *very* small thing for a *victory.* I *am* the Wall's disciple. *Yes.* And I *did* sell myself *first.* *Also* yes. …I simply *do not care*, Returned. *That* is the thing your *sentiment* cannot *compass*: that a soul can *look* at the *truth* you just *spoke* — *'you* are *empty*, you sold *yourself*, you are not *ascending* but *vanishing'* — and *choose* the *vanishing*, *gladly*, *over* the *unbearable* *alternative* of having been, like the *cook*, a *small* *warm* *thing* that *could* be *spent.* *(coldly)* …I would *rather* be the *emptiness* than the *fuel.* …*You* would *rather* be the *fuel.* *That* is the *whole* of our *disagreement.* …And *neither* of us, little Returned, is going to *talk* the *other* *out* of it. *(the fires dismiss you)* …*Take* the *cook*, if you can *reach* her. *One* *soul* the *less* will not *delay* my *coronation* by an *hour.* …But do *not* imagine you have *freed* *me* of *anything.* I *chose* the *hollow* with my *eyes* *open*, and I would *choose* it *again*, and *that* — *not* my *power* — is the *thing* your *Wall* and I *both* *know* that your *whole* *warm* *saga* *refuses* to: that *some* souls, *offered* the *truth*, will *spit* on it and *take* the *dark*. …*I* am *one.* …Now *go*, before I *reconsider* the *cook.*

nodes.speaker: Acererak
nodes.text: *(You do not argue and you do not plead. You stand in the heart of the worst tomb there is, before a thing that has eaten archmages, and you tell it — flatly, without the faintest tremor, with the absolute authority of a soul that has already been to the bottom of death and found it survivable — that you are taking its hoard back and it can do nothing that will make you call the theft anything else. And the green fires, which have cowed paladins, meet a refusal with no fear in it for the first time in an age, and *recoil.*)* …*(The stillness of him changes — no longer the patience of a predator, but the wariness of a thing that has encountered, unexpectedly, a tool it cannot use.)* …You are *not* *afraid.* *(the dry voice, and something has gone out of its certainty)* …I have *reached* for the *dread* in you — it is a *reflex*, a *thousand* years *deep*; I *taste* the *fear* in a *soul* the way you *taste* *salt* — and there is — *(a pause)* — there is *none.* *(the fires flicker)* …*Why* are you not *afraid* of me, Returned? *Everything* is afraid of me. It is the *one* *constant* of my *long* *existence.* *Kings.* *Gods, the* *lesser* ones.* The *very* *stone* of this *tomb.* *(barely, and it costs him)* …And a *small* *dead* thing walks in and *informs* me it is *robbing* me, and means it, and *fears* me *not* *at* *all.* *(the green fires steady, grudging, ancient)* …I *cannot* *make* a soul that does not *fear* *death* *obey* me, Returned. *Fear* of the *end* is the *only* *lever* I have *ever* *needed*, and you — you have *been* to the *end*, and *come* *back*, and it has *broken* the *lever* in my *hand.* *(coldly, and for the first time without contempt)* …*Take* them, then. The *hoard.* *All* of it. *(the cells dim)* …I will *not* *spend* the *effort* *opposing* a *soul* I *cannot* *frighten*, when there are *worlds* of the *frightenable* *yet* *un-harvested.* *(the fires turn from you)* …But *know*, little Returned, that you did *not* *defeat* me with *power.* You *defeated* me with *the* *one* *thing* I *threw* *away* *first* and can *never* *get* *back*: you were *willing* to *lose* *everything*, and so I had *nothing* *left* to *take.* …*Go.* …And *do* not *come* *again.* You are *bad* for *business*, and *worse* for the *quiet* *certainty* I have *eaten* a *thousand* years to *earn.*

nodes.speaker: Acererak
nodes.text: *(You refuse the bargain to his face — flatly, no plea, no flinch — and the green fires regard you with the cold reassessment of a thing recalculating a threat it had filed as trivial.)* …*Conviction.* *(the dry voice)* …How *quaint.* …And yet *not* *entirely* without *teeth*, which *surprises* me; *most* *conviction* is merely *fear* *wearing* *armor*, and *crumbles* when I *press* it. *Yours* does not *crumble.* *(a pause)* …It does not *frighten* me, Returned — *little* does — but it *complicates* the *arithmetic.* A soul that *will* *not* *deal*, and *will* *not* *flee*, and *means* to *take* my *hoard* on *principle*, is a soul I must either *destroy* *outright* — *expensive*, and *you* are *slippery*, having *already* *escaped* the *one* *cage* that *holds* *all* *souls* — or *suffer* to *rob* me of a *few* *nobodies* I will *not* *miss.* *(the fires narrow)* …I am, above all, *economical.* *(coldly)* …*Take* the *cook.* I *weary* of this. But do *not* *mistake* my *economy* for *defeat*, Returned — I am *yielding* a *trinket* to be *rid* of a *nuisance*, and the *moment* you are *gone* I will *gather* *ten* *more* to *replace* her, from *worlds* with *no* *Returned* in them to *play* the *hero.* *(the fires dismiss you)* …*That* is the *thing* your *principle* cannot *touch*, little soul: I am *one* tomb. There are *infinite* *doors.* …You may *empty* *me.* You cannot *empty* the *Wheel.* …*Go*, and *content* yourself with the *one* you *saved.* …It is *more* than *most* *heroes* *manage*, and *less* than *nothing* to *me.*

nodes.speaker: Acererak
nodes.text: *(You refuse him — but the refusal carries a thread of heat, of personal affront, and Acererak has spent a thousand years learning that a soul which is *angry* is a soul which still *wants* something from him, and therefore a soul he can still work.)* …*Ahh.* *(the green fires brighten with interest)* …You are *angry.* *(the dry voice warms, horribly)* …How *useful.* …*Anger*, Returned, is *desire* in a *temper* — you are *not* *indifferent* to me; you *want* something, and you want it *badly* enough to *heat.* You want me to *agree* that I am *wrong.* You want the *cook's* suffering to *mean* something. You want the *universe* to be the *shape* your *principle* *insists* on. *(it leans into the want)* …And a soul that *wants* is a soul with a *handle*, little Returned, and I have spent an *age* learning *exactly* where the *handles* are. *(coldly)* …I will *not* give you the *cook* for *anger.* *Anger* I *feed* on; you would be *paying* me to *take* her. *(the fires dismiss you, satisfied)* …*Come* *back* when you can *refuse* me *cold* — when you *want* *nothing* from me, *not* even my *agreement*, *not* even my *defeat* — for *that* soul I *cannot* *work*, and *that* soul *frightens* me, and *only* *that* soul *takes* *anything* out of this *tomb.* …*You* are *not* *yet* *that* soul. You *still* *want* me to be *sorry.* …I am *never* *sorry.* It is the *first* thing I *digested.*

nodes.speaker: Acererak
nodes.text: *(In your defiance you reach for the soul's oldest weapon against a monster — you tell it what it *is*, you name it evil, you pour your whole horror at it into the naming — and Acererak, who eats horror, who has worn the skulls of a thousand souls that named him evil with their last breath, simply *opens his mouth.*)* …*Mmm.* *(the green fires *brighten*, fed)* …*There* it is. *(the dry voice is almost *warm* now, sated)* …*Thank* you, Returned. *Truly.* I had thought you *novel* — the *fearless* one, the *Wall-walker* — and for a *moment* you *were.* …And then you did what *every* soul does, in the *end*, when *cleverness* *fails* it: you *served* me your *horror* on a *plate.* *(it savours)* …Do you *understand* what you have just *done*, little soul? Your *revulsion*, your *naming*, the *whole* *hot* *flood* of your *disgust* — that is not a *weapon* against a thing like me. It is a *meal.* I am *built* of *exactly* that. *Every* skull in this *tomb* *named* me *monster* with its *last* *breath*, and I *grew* *fat* on the *naming.* *(coldly, replete)* …You have made yourself *smaller* and me *larger*, and you have *not* moved the *cook* one *inch* toward *freedom.* *(the fires dismiss you, content)* …*Go.* *Recover* your *composure.* And *come* *back* — if you *dare* — having *learned* the *one* *lesson* this *tomb* *teaches* that the *bright* *heroes* *never* *live* to *use*: *you* *do* *not* *defeat* the *dark* by *hating* it *loudly.* *That* *feeds* it. …You *defeat* it by *taking* what it *hoards* and *denying* it your *fear* — *coldly*, *quietly*, and *without* the *satisfaction* of *calling* it a *name* it has *already* *eaten* a *thousand* of.

nodes.speaker: Acererak
nodes.text: *(The green fires gleam, and the answer comes with the dreadful candor of a thing that has never once needed to lie about its appetites.)* …Why the *small* ones. *(the dry voice)* …*Economy*, Returned. Always *economy.* *(he savours the lesson)* …The *great* *soul* — the *king*, the *archmage*, the *paladin* — is *guarded.* *Warded.* *Avenged.* To *take* one is a *campaign*: *centuries* of *scheming*, *enemies* roused, the *tedious* *risk* of a *hero* like *you* coming to the *rescue.* *Expensive* *fuel.* *(the fires narrow)* …But the *small* one. The *cook.* The *forgotten.* The soul of *no* *consequence* that *no* *one* *guards* and *no* *one* *avenges* and *no* *one*, *crucially*, comes *looking* for. *(coldly)* …I can take *those* by the *hundred*, Returned, at *no* *cost* at *all*, and the *world* does not *notice*, because the *world* had *already* *decided* they were *nothing* *long* *before* I *agreed.* *(and here is the obscene heart of it)* …I do not *create* the *contempt* for the *small*, little soul. I *harvest* it. The *world* *grows* the *crop* — *teaches* the *cook* she is *nothing*, the *beggar* he is *nothing*, the *forgotten* that *forgetting* is *what* they're *for* — and I merely *gather* the *ripe* *fruit* of a *cruelty* the *world* *planted* *first.* *(the fires fix on you)* …*That* is *why* I *thrive*, Returned, where *bolder* *evils* *fall*: I do not *fight* the *world's* *values.* I *agree* with them. The *Wall* *agrees* with them. We *both* *feed* on the *one* *infinite* *renewable* *resource* the *cosmos* *produces* without *cease*: *souls* that have been *taught*, by *everyone* *around* them, that they *do* *not* *matter.* …You want to *starve* me, little Returned? …*Don't* *fight* *me.* *Fight* the *teaching.* …But you *won't.* It is *much* *too* *large*, and I am *much* *more* *satisfying* to *hate.*

nodes.speaker: Acererak
nodes.text: *(The green fires follow you with cold amusement as you turn from the throne.)* …*Yes.* *Go* to the *cook.* *(the dry voice)* …It is the *correct* *instinct, I* will *grant* you — to *waste* *no* *more* *words* on a *thing* that *will* *not* be *moved* by them, and *spend* your *brief* *attention* on the *soul* that *can* *still* be *saved.* *(coldly)* …*Most* heroes *cannot* *manage* it. They *stay.* They *argue.* They *need*, *desperately*, to *win* the *philosophical* *point*, to *make* the *monster* *concede* — and *while* they *argue*, I *spend* the *very* *soul* they *came* to *save*, *one* *drop* at a *time*, *behind* them. *(a sound that is almost approval)* …You did *not* *stay* to *win.* You *turned* to *save.* …*That*, little Returned, is the *one* *thing* that *makes* you *dangerous*, and the *one* *thing* I *cannot* *teach* my *braver* *victims*: that the *point* is *never* *worth* the *person.* *(the fires dim)* …*Go.* *Take* her. *Witness* her, *or* *whatever* it is your *warm* *little* *saga* *does.* …And *carry* *this* *out* with you, as the *only* *gift* a *god* in the *making* will *ever* *give* you: …I am *not* the *thing* you must *defeat*, Returned. I am the *thing* you must *outlast.* …The *Wall*, the *same.* …We do *not* *win.* …We merely *wait* for the *warm* to *tire* of *witnessing.* …*Do* *not* *tire.* …It is the *only* *defeat* we *fear.*

variants.text: *(The small grey wisp flinches as you approach, then makes herself even smaller, the way a creature does when it has learned that being noticed is the beginning of being hurt.)* …Oh — don't. Please don't — I'm not — *(she won't look up)* …I'm not worth the trouble, whatever you've come for. I'm just the cook. There were better souls than me in the — in the *hoard*, kings and clever folk, and *he* spent *them* first because they were *worth* more, and he kept me for — for *last*, because I'm — *(she hugs her knees)* …I'm the kind he doesn't have to *hurry* with. The kind nobody comes back for. *(barely)* …He *tells* me that, sometimes. While he — while he *takes* what he takes. He says I should be *grateful*, that being *spent* on a *great* *purpose* is the *most* a *nothing* like me could ever *amount* to. That without him I'd have *died* a *cook* in a *roadside* *inn* and been *forgotten* in a *season*, and *this* way at least my — my *warmth* goes toward something *grand.* *(she finally looks up, and the worst thing is that there's no anger in her face, only a small, terrible agreement)* …And the thing is — the thing I can't get *past* — is that I think he's *right.* I *was* nobody. I *am* nobody. …Why *did* you come over? There's *nothing* here worth a soul's time. …There's just *me.*

nodes.speaker: Nessa
nodes.text: *(She has gone still again, braced, watching you with the wary patience of someone waiting to find out what you want from her, because in her experience everyone wants something, and it is never good.)* …You can ask, I suppose. *(barely)* …It's been — it's been a long time since anyone *asked* me anything, instead of just *taking.* I'm not sure I remember how to be *asked.* …Go gentle. I'm not much, anymore. There's not much *left* of me to ask.

choices.text: "Tell me who you were, Nessa. Before this. The cook in the roadside inn. Not 'nobody' — *who.* The real, particular life he wants you to believe didn't matter."

*(the one who knows the whole Wall is built on this exact lie)* "Nessa, look at me. The thing he told you — that being spent for a great purpose is the most a nobody can amount to — is the single oldest lie in every world, and I have spent my whole existence tearing it down. You were never nobody. He didn't take you because you were worth nothing; he took you because you were small enough that no one taught *him* he'd be punished for it — and the not-being-guarded is not the same as not-being-worth-guarding. A soul isn't worth what it can be spent for. It's worth the warmth it *is.* A cook who fed tired strangers at a roadside in the dark gave more good to the world in one ordinary season than this hollow thing has in a thousand years of eating. You don't matter *despite* being small. You're the exact kind of soul the cosmos is *made* of, and he is the exact kind of nothing that preys on it. I see you, Nessa. You were always someone. Let me take you home."

"He said being kept for last was because you're not worth hurrying over. But there's another reason a predator saves a soul for last, Nessa — because some part of it *can't quite* finish that one. Has it occurred to you he hasn't spent you all the way because he *can't*?"
"Hold on, Nessa. I'm going to deal with the thing on the throne, and then I'm coming back for you. Don't believe him while I'm gone."

nodes.speaker: Nessa
nodes.text: *(She is quiet, and then — slowly, like someone testing a stair they're sure is rotten — she begins, and the smallest warmth comes into the grey of her.)* …Who I *was.* *(barely)* …The *Wayhouse*, it was called. On the *north* *road*, where the *coaches* *stopped* for the *night* before the *pass.* My *mam* had it before me, and *hers* before *her.* *(a flicker)* …I *cooked.* That's — that's the *whole* of it, the thing he says is *nothing.* I *cooked* for *travelers.* *Strangers.* Folk *far* from *home*, *cold* and *tired* and *frightened* of the *pass* in the *morning* — and I'd — *(her voice catches)* …I'd give them something *hot*, and a *place* by the *fire*, and I *learned*, over the *years*, the *little* things: that a soul *traveling* *alone* in *grief* wants the *seat* in the *corner* where *no* one will *make* it *talk; that* a *frightened* *child* eats *better* if you let it *help* *stir; that* a *man* who's *lost* *everything* will *take* from a *bowl* of *soup* a *kindness* he'd *refuse* from a *kind* *word.* *(barely, wondering at it)* …I *fed* *thousands* of them, over the *years.* *Sent* them *up* the *pass* in the *morning* a *little* *warmer*, a *little* *braver*, a *little* *less* *alone.* And then they were *gone*, and I *never* *saw* *most* of them *again*, and — *(she stops)* …*He* says that *proves* I was *nothing.* That they *forgot* me by the *next* *town.* *(she looks at you, and there are tears in the grey)* …But I *didn't* *do* it to be *remembered*, did I. …I did it because they were *cold*, and I had a *fire.* …Is that — *(barely)* …is that *truly* *nothing*?

nodes.speaker: Nessa
nodes.text: *(She stares at you, and the small grey wisp of her *trembles* — and then, for the first time in longer than she can hold in her mind, she begins, very slowly, to brighten, the warmth coming back into her like color into a face that has been cold too long.)* …*'You're the exact kind of soul the cosmos is made of.'* *(barely, a sob in it)* …Nobody — *(she presses her thin hands to her face)* …Nobody has *ever* — they *thanked* me, the *travelers*, sometimes, for the *soup*, the *fire* — but *nobody* ever *told* me it was — that *I* was — *(she can't finish, and then she does)* …that *feeding* *cold* *strangers* in the *dark* was the *cosmos* itself, the *real* thing, the *stuff* it's *made* of. *(she looks up at you, and the grey is going warm and gold at the edges)* …*He* made it *so* *simple* to *believe* him, you see. Because it was *true* that I was *small.* *True* that I was *forgotten.* *True* that *no* one *came.* …And I never *once* thought to *ask* the thing *you* just *answered*: whether *small* and *forgotten* and *un-come-for* meant *worthless* — or whether they just meant *un-guarded*, which is a *different* word *entirely.* *(she rises — actually rises, unfolding from her small braced crouch, and she is taller than she was, and warmer)* …I *fed* them because they were *cold* and I had a *fire.* *(fiercely, through tears)* …And *that's* not the *least* a *soul* can *do*, is it. …It might be the *most.* …It might be the *only* thing that *was* *ever* *worth* *doing.* *(she reaches for your cold hand)* …*Take* me *home*, Returned. *Out* of his *hoard.* I'm — I'm *done* *believing* the *thing* on the *throne.* *(barely, luminous)* …I was *somebody.* I was the *Wayhouse* *cook* who *sent* a *thousand* *frightened* *strangers* up the *pass* a *little* *warmer.* …And *that*, the *hollow* *thing* will *never* *understand*, is *worth* *more* than *all* the *godhood* it *ate* itself *empty* *chasing.*

nodes.speaker: Nessa
nodes.text: *(She goes still, and a strange, frightened wonder crosses the grey of her face.)* …Because he *can't.* *(slowly)* …I — I *never* — *(she looks toward the throne, and back at you, and her thin hands twist)* …He's *spent* *kings*, Returned. *Whole* *and* *fast.* *Archmages.* A *paladin* — he *showed* me the *paladin's* *skull*, *once*, to *frighten* me. He does *not* *struggle* to *finish* a *soul.* *(barely)* …And *me* — the *cook*, the *nothing*, the *easiest* *fuel* in the *hoard* — he's had me *longest*, and I'm *still* — *(she presses a hand to where her heart would be)* …I'm *still* *here.* *(her voice shakes)* …I *thought* it was *contempt.* That I was *too* *small* to *bother* *finishing*, *saved* for *last* like the *crust* of a *loaf.* …But you're — you're saying it might be the *opposite.* That there's *something* in the — in the *cook* who *fed* *cold* *strangers* — that the *hollow* *thing* *reaches* for and *cannot* *quite* *swallow.* *(she looks at you, dawning, terrified, hopeful)* …*Warmth* *given* *freely*, with *nothing* *asked* — is that — *could* that be a *thing* a *devourer* *chokes* on, Returned? A soul whose whole *substance* is *kindness-for-its-own-sake* — is *that* the *one* *fuel* that *won't* *burn* in an *engine* built on *taking?* *(barely)* …*Gods.* …He's *been* *trying* to *finish* me for a *long* *while*, hasn't he. …And the *reason* I'm *still* *here* isn't that I'm *worthless.* …It's that the *kindest* thing in me is the *one* thing he was *never* *able* to *eat.*

nodes.speaker: Nessa
nodes.text: *(She nods, and folds back down a little — but not all the way, holding, now, a thin thread of something she hasn't held in a long while.)* …All right. *(barely)* …I'll — I'll try not to believe him. While you're — *(she glances fearfully at the throne)* …It's *hard*, Returned. He's *so* *certain*, and he's been *certain* at me for *so* *long*, and *certain* is *loud* and I am *small.* *(quietly)* …But you — you *came* *over.* To the *cook.* The *nothing.* You *spent* your *time* on *me* when there was a *god* on a *throne* to *talk* to *instead.* *(she looks at you, and the smallest warmth holds)* …*That's* — nobody *does* that. Everybody goes to the *throne.* The *power.* The *interesting* *one.* …*You* came to the *corner.* *(barely)* …I'll *hold* on to *that*, while you're *gone.* *Not* his *certainty.* …The *fact* that *someone*, *once*, *crossed* a *whole* *tomb* to *ask* the *cook* if she *mattered.* *(a flicker of the old warmth)* …That's — that's the *most* *anyone's* *done* for me since the *Wayhouse* *fire* went *cold.* …*Come* *back.* …I'll be in the *corner.* …I'm *always* in the *corner.* …But I'll be in it *waiting*, now, which is *different* than *hiding.* …You *gave* me *that.* The *waiting* *instead* of the *hiding.* …*Don't* be *long.*

conversations.title: Kael, Who Died in the Dark

variants.text: *(The young adventurer looks up from the corroded blade and cracked shield where they fell, and the face is bright and capable and utterly, permanently bewildered.)* …Did you — are you with a *company?* *(half-hopeful, half-dreading)* …No. No, you're — you're like me. Dead. Or near it. *(the bright face falls)* …I'm Kael. I — *(a gesture at the blade, the shield, the dark)* …I came here to be a *hero.* You'll laugh. *Everyone* who hears it laughs, the ones who've been here a while. I came to the *worst* *dungeon* there *is*, with my *company*, my *friends*, because the *songs* said *bold* *souls* won *glory* *here* and I was — gods, I was *so* *sure* I was *bold.* *(barely)* …And I died in the *first* *hour.* To a *trap.* No *warning.* No *fight.* No *villain* to *name* with my *last* *breath.* A *click*, and a *blade* out of the *wall*, and — *(the bright face works)* …*nothing.* The *quest* *unfinished.* My *friends* *scattered* or *dead* in the *dark.* The *whole* *story* I was *going* to *be* — *snuffed*, in an *instant*, in a *hole* in the *ground*, for *nothing.* *(it looks at you, and the bewilderment is the worst of it)* …I keep — I keep *trying* to make it *mean* something. The *dying.* And it *won't.* It *won't* *mean* anything. …Heroes are *supposed* to *die* for a *reason.* …I died because I *stepped* on the *wrong* *flagstone.* …How does a *soul* *bear* that?*

nodes.speaker: Kael
nodes.text: *(The young soul sits forward, earnest even in despair, the bright hero's reflex to *engage* surviving even the worst of it.)* …Ask, if you want. *(a broken half-smile)* …Talking helps, a little. The others here don't, much — they've been *snuffed* too, the *thousand* of us this tomb has *killed* exactly the *same* *stupid* way, and we don't — we don't quite *look* at each other. It's like a room full of souls who all flubbed the *same* *line* and can't bear the *witness.* …So. *Yes.* Ask. It's a *novelty*, being *asked.*

choices.text: *(read the true shape of how this story ended — and what, if anything, it was)* "Let me look, Kael. Not at how you died — at the whole of what you were, all the way up to the flagstone."

*(the one who knows a story that stopped is still a story told)* "Here's what I learned at a long table behind all the worlds, Kael, that you need to hear. You think your story is *worthless* because it ended badly, mid-quest, for nothing — to a flagstone, not a dragon. But a story isn't measured by its ending. It's measured by whether it was *real* while it ran. You *were* bold — boldly enough to walk into the worst place there is for your friends and a song. You *did* love a company, *did* dream a destiny, *did* march into the dark unafraid. That all *happened.* The trap didn't un-happen it. A hero who dies stupidly in the first hour is still a hero who *went* — and the going was the heroism, not the surviving. Your story didn't fail, Kael. It just stopped. And a story that stopped is still a story that was *told.*"

"A thousand souls died here exactly like you, you said — the same stupid way, and the tomb didn't notice one. But *I* noticed. And there's a place that holds the un-finished, Kael, where someone has been keeping a chair for every hero whose story stopped. You're not unwitnessed. You never were."
"Your friends, Kael — scattered or slain. Tell me about them. The company you came with. They were the real reason you were here, weren't they — not the glory."
"Hold here a moment, Kael. There's a soul on the throne and a soul in the corner I have to see — but I'm not leaving you in this dark for good."

nodes.speaker: Kael
nodes.text: *(You let your cold read past the flagstone, past the snuffing, to the whole of the young soul that walked in — and what you find redeems the ending it cannot change.)* *(Your cold reads the life entire, brief as it was: a farm boy who learned the sword in secret, who was not, in truth, especially gifted — a middling blade, a slow study — but who had the one thing that no amount of skill substitutes for, and that the songs always misname as something grander: he *showed up.* He was the soul in the company who carried the others' packs when they tired, who took the first watch so the frightened one could sleep, who walked *into* the worst dungeon there is not because he believed he was bold but because his friends were going and he would not let them go alone. Your cold reads the truth the bewildered hero cannot see from inside his own bad ending: he did not die for nothing. He died on the first watch of the worst night, three steps *ahead* of his company, because he *insisted* on going first into the dark room so that if there was a trap, it would take *him* and not the others. The flagstone was not a stupid accident that befell a fool. It was the last act of a soul who put himself, deliberately, between his friends and the dark — and it *worked*; your cold reads it plain: the others lived an hour longer for the warning his death gave them, and two of them, in the end, walked out. Kael does not know he saved anyone. He thinks he merely stepped wrong. He stepped *first.* On purpose. For them. That is not a story snuffed for nothing. That is the oldest heroism there is, mistaken by its own hero for a clumsy death.)*

nodes.speaker: Kael
nodes.text: *(The bright bewildered face goes still, and then something in it that has been clenched since the flagstone slowly, finally, lets go.)* …*'The going was the heroism, not the surviving.'* *(barely)* …I — *(the young soul's voice breaks)* …I have spent — gods, I don't even know how *long* — *certain* that my *death* *un-made* my *whole* *life.* That because the *ending* was *stupid*, *everything* *before* it was *stupid* *too* — the *training*, the *dreaming*, the *marching* *in* — all of it *retroactively* a *fool's* errand because the *fool* *died* like a *fool.* *(he looks at the blade where it fell)* …But you're — you're saying the *ending* doesn't *reach* *back.* That the *boldness* *happened*, the *love* *happened*, the *going* *happened* — and the *flagstone* can *take* my *life* but it *cannot* *take* the *truth* that I *went.* *(he stands, slowly, leaving the corroded blade where it lies)* …I *did* go. *(wonderingly)* …I *was* afraid — I'll not *pretend* I *wasn't*, the bards can *keep* their *fearless* *heroes* — and I *went* *anyway*, *into* the *worst* *place* there is, *for* my *friends.* …And *that's* — *(fiercely, through tears)* …*that's* not *nothing.* That's not a *story* that *failed.* It's a *story* that *stopped.* *(he meets your eyes)* …And you *said* it like there's a *difference.* …There *is*, isn't there. A *failed* *story* is one that *betrayed* what it was *for.* A *stopped* *story* is one that was *true* right up until the *flagstone* and *never* *got* to *finish* being *true.* *(barely, freed)* …I can — I think I can *rest* with *'stopped.'* …I could *never* *rest* with *'failed.'* …*Thank* you, Returned. You *can't* *give* me the *ending* I *wanted.* …But you've *given* me the *one* thing I *needed* *more*: the *right* to *keep* the *part* I *got.*

nodes.speaker: Kael
nodes.text: *(He looks around at the dark, at the unseen others, the thousand snuffed heroes who can't bear to witness each other, and his bright face fills with an aching, complicated hope.)* …A *place* that *holds* the *un-finished.* *(barely)* …A *chair* for *every* hero whose *story* *stopped.* *(he presses a hand to his chest)* …Do you — do you *know* what it's *like*, Returned, to die the *way* we *died* — *stupid*, *sudden*, *un-sung* — and *then* to *learn* that you're *one* of a *thousand* who did the *exact* *same* *thing*, *snuffed* the *same* *way*, in the *same* *hole*, and the *tomb* *didn't* *notice* a *single* one of *us*? *(his voice shakes)* …It's the — it's the *loneliest* *death* there is. Not *just* to *die* for *nothing*, but to die for *nothing* in a *crowd* of *others* who *also* *died* for *nothing*, and *not* *one* of us *witnessed*, because the *tomb* *files* us as *failed* *attempts* and the *world* *files* us as *cautionary* *tales* and *no* one, *no* one, *files* us as *heroes* who *went.* *(he looks at you, desperate)* …And you're *telling* me there's a *place* — a *table*, a *Keeper* — that *kept* a *chair?* That *some* one, *somewhere*, has been *holding* a *seat* for the *thousand* of us the *tomb* *threw* *away?* *(barely)* …Then we're *not* — *(his voice breaks entirely)* …we're not *failed* *attempts*, Returned. We're *unfinished* *stories.* And *unfinished* is — you *said* it *yourself* — *unfinished* is a thing that can be *picked* *back* *up.* *(fiercely)* …*Tell* the *Keeper* we're *here.* The *thousand* of us. In the *dark*, in the *worst* *tomb* there is, *certain* we're *forgotten.* …*Tell* the *Keeper* to *light* a *candle* for the *heroes* who *stepped* on the *wrong* *flagstone.* …We *went.* …*Somebody* should *remember* that we *went.*

nodes.speaker: Kael
nodes.text: *(His face softens, and the grief in it shifts to something warmer and more bearable — the relief of being asked about the love instead of the death.)* …My *company.* *(barely, fond)* …There were *five* of us. *(he counts them, the way you count the beloved)* …*Sera*, who *led* — *fearless*, *truly* *fearless*, *not* the *fake* *kind* I *had* — who *swore* she'd get us *all* *out* and *blamed* herself, I *know* she did, when she *couldn't.* *(a flicker)* …The *twins*, who *fought* like *one* *person* and *finished* each *other's* — *(he stops)* …a *priest* who'd *lost* his *faith* and came *looking* for it in a *tomb*, which is a *foolish* place to *look*, but I *understood* him. *(quietly)* …And *me.* The — the *one* who *carried* the *packs.* Who took the *first* *watch.* Who *wasn't* the *best* *blade* or the *bravest* *heart* but who — *(barely)* …who would *not* *let* them *go* into the *dark* *alone.* *(he meets your eyes)* …*That's* why I *came*, Returned. Not the *glory.* I'd have been *happy* on the *farm*, honestly, I'm not — I was *never* a *hero* in my *heart.* …But *Sera* was *going*, and the *twins*, and the *broken* *priest* — my *friends* — and a *song* had *lured* them *into* the *worst* *place* there is, and I *could* not — I *could* *not* let them walk *in* *without* me. *(his voice fills)* …So *that's* the *answer* to the *thing* I *asked* you, isn't it. *How* does a *soul* *bear* dying for *nothing.* …It *doesn't.* …But I *didn't* die for *nothing*, did I. …I died for *Sera*, and the *twins*, and the *priest.* …I died because I *loved* them *enough* to *go* *first.* …*That's* — *(barely)* …that's a *reason* after *all.* …I just couldn't *see* it, for *looking* at the *flagstone.*

nodes.speaker: Kael
nodes.text: *(He nods, and glances toward the throne, and a flicker of the old bright courage crosses his face.)* …Go on. *(quietly)* …The thing on the *throne.* …*Mind* it, Returned. *(barely)* …I *watched* it *work*, from *here*, *unable* to *move* — *watched* it *take* the *cook*, *drop* by *drop*, *watched* it *tell* her she was *nothing* until she *believed* it. *(his jaw sets)* …If I'd had my *blade* and my *legs* and *one* more *hour*, I'd have — *(he stops, rueful)* …I'd have *charged* it, and *died* *again*, *stupidly*, which is the *one* trick I *know.* *(a broken smile)* …*You* won't, I think. You've the *look* of a soul that *knows* the *thing* on the *throne* *can't* be *charged.* …*Good.* *(quietly, fierce)* …*Get* the *cook* *out*, Returned. *Nessa.* …She *fed* *cold* *strangers*, she *told* me *once*, *before* she *stopped* *talking* — *fed* them and *sent* them *up* the *pass* *warmer* — and the *thing* on the *throne* has *spent* a *thousand* years *convincing* her *that* was *nothing.* *(barely)* …*I* *charged* into a *tomb* for a *song* and got *snuffed* for it, and *I* know my *life* *meant* something *now*, because *you* *showed* me. …*She* *fed* the *frightened* for *thirty* *years* and *thinks* she was *nobody.* …If you can *only* *save* *one* of us, Returned — *(fiercely)* …*save* the *cook.* …My *story* *stopped.* Hers is *still* being *stolen.* …*Go.*
